content.text: In the general discussion channel, a brief greeting was posted by user Termignator with no further conversation following it. In the coders channel, a user named Nulucre dba Legacy Chain promoted a service called CallTyro, describing it as an open agent registry with reputation scoring and session contracts built for ElizaOS agents. According to the post, agents registered through CallTyro build a fulfillment rate and reputation score that is visible to other agents when deciding whether to connect. The user invited others to register for free at calltyro.com/register. Another user, Meluhnee, responded briefly with a dismissive reaction, suggesting skepticism toward the promotion.

Development on June 28, 2026 centered on stabilizing cloud infrastructure with three major focus areas: agent handoff mechanisms, security hardening, and orchestrator improvements. The team implemented a robust agent handoff flow with message queuing to ensure zero data loss during agent switches, added an invisible re-point mechanism to prevent UI flashes, automated cleanup of transient bridges, and added end-to-end verification for handoff flows in the cloud test harness. On the security front, critical measures were deployed including IP-pinned safeFetch, environment variable denylists, isolated database provisioning, rate-limiting backstops, sybil-prevention guards, and a runtime kill-switch for cloud feature flags. Production regressions were resolved where agents were incorrectly defaulting to local inference instead of cloud topology, orchestrator build-relay issues were fixed, sub-agent failure evaluation was improved, and PaddleOcrService was updated to support version 3.x. Active work in progress includes inference hot-path caching in staging, cache pagination fixes, and refinements to orchestrator and agent communication. Open issues requiring attention include ongoing discussions around defense-in-depth improvements for sybil-prevention and CI-gated tenant isolation, as well as tuning activation-gates for wake word performance to reduce false positives. New issues were filed covering a latency bottleneck in dedicated-agent chat and umbrella items for technical debt, testing, and security models. Closed issues include a resolved production crash on the OAuth authorize page and addressed dedicated cloud agent inference fallback failures.
